'''Ryan Brierley''' (born 12 March 1992) is a former [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a [[Goal (sports)#Rugby football|goal]]-kicking {{rlp|fb|sh}} or {{rlp|so}} for [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] in the [[RFL Championship]]. He is the currently the [[Chief executive officer]] of [[Salford Red Devils]].

He previously played for the [[Leigh Centurions]] over two separate spells in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] and in the [[Super League]]. Brierley has also played for [[Salford Red Devils]], [[Huddersfield Giants]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers|Hull KR]] in the [[Super League|top flight]] and [[Toronto Wolfpack]] and [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] in the lower tiers, and spent time on loan from [[Toronto Wolfpack|Toronto]] at [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] in the second tier.

==Background==
Brierley was born in [[Preston, Lancashire]], England. After moving to [[Bolton, Greater Manchester|Bolton]] as a two-year old, Brierley played junior rugby with local club Westhoughton Lions.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://outofyourleague.podbean.com/e/ooyl-with-salford-red-devils-ryan-brierley/|accessdate=24 August 2023|date=23 August 2023|title=Out of Your League with Salford Red Devils' Ryan Brierley}}</ref>

==Club career==
===Early career===
Brierley joined [[Castleford Tigers]] academy in 2009, and appeared in a first team friendly for Castleford early in [[2012 Castleford Tigers season|2012]], scoring a try against [[York City Knights]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===Leigh Centurions===
After failing to get an opportunity in the first team with Castleford, Brierley joined [[Leigh Centurions]]. Brierley's first appearance for the club came on Saturday 7 January 2012 when he scored two tries in a friendly victory away to Super League neighbours [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]].

He became a prolific try-scorer at the club, finishing as the club's top try-scorer for four consecutive seasons between 2012 and 2015. He moved into the club's top 5 all-time leading try-scorers with 133 in 125 appearances following a two-try display against [[Bradford Bulls]] on 28 February 2016, which was his last match for the club.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.leighjournal.co.uk/sport/leighcenturions/14309857.Turley_praises_Brierley_s_achievement_in_reaching_top_five_try_scorers/|title=Turley praises Brierley's achievement in reaching top five try scorers|website=Leigh Journal}}</ref>

===Huddersfield===
After triggering a clause in his contract, Brierley left Leigh and joined [[Super League]] club [[Huddersfield Giants]] on a four-and-a-half-year contract in March 2016.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/ryan-brierley-leaves-leigh-join-11076260|title=Ryan Brierley leaves Leigh to join Huddersfield Giants|website=Manchester Evening News}}</ref>

===Toronto Wolfpack===
Brierley signed with the [[Toronto Wolfpack]] for an undisclosed transfer fee in April 2017.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/10844131/toronto-wolfpack-sign-huddersfield-giants-and-scotland-international-ryan-brierley|title=Toronto Wolfpack sign Huddersfield Giants and Scotland international Ryan Brierley|date=20 April 2017|accessdate=24 August 2023|website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
Brierley signed with [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in December 2019.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11879511/hull-kingston-rovers-sign-jamie-ellis-and-ryan-brierley|title=Hull Kingston Rovers sign Jamie Ellis and Ryan Brierley|accessdate=24 August 2023|date=6 December 2019|website=Sky Sports}}</ref> Rovers finished bottom of the table, avoiding relegation following former club Toronto's exit from the competition.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2020/jul/20/toronto-wolfpack-exit-super-league-season-casting-doubt-on-clubs-future?CMP=Share_AndroidApp_Other|title=Toronto Wolfpack exit Super League season|accessdate=24 August 2023|date=20 July 2020|website=The Guardian}}</ref>

===Leigh Centurions (rejoined)===
After just one season with Hull KR, Brierley returned to Leigh for the [[2021 Super League season|2021 season]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ryan-brierley-returns-to-leigh-2/|title=Ryan Brierley returns to Leigh|date=30 Oct 2020|accessdate=24 Dec 2020|website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

In July, Brierley was ruled out indefinitely with an eye injury,<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.leighjournal.co.uk/sport/leighcenturions/19428328.leighs-ryan-brierley-see-specialist-horrific-eye-injury/|title=Leigh's Ryan Brierley to see specialist about horrific eye injury|website=Leigh Journal|date=8 July 2021 }}</ref> but returned before the end of the season to score a [[hat-trick]] against [[Wakefield Trinity]], Leigh's second victory of the season.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58532664|title=Leigh Centurions 26-18 Wakefield Trinity|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

===Salford Red Devils===
On 20 September 2021, Brierley signed for [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] in the [[Super League]], the club he supported as a child.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=20 Sep 2021|accessdate=31 Dec 2021|title=Ryan Brierley to join boyhood club Salford|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ryan-brierley-to-join-boyhood-club-salford/}}</ref>
Brierley played 26 games for Salford in the 2022 Super League season including the clubs semi-final loss against St Helens. In the 2023 Super League season, Brierley played 26 games for Salford as the club finished 7th on the table. Brierley played 23 games for Salford in the 2024 Super League season including the clubs elimination play-off loss against Leigh.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.salfordnow.co.uk/2023/10/02/salford-red-devils-2023-season-in-review/|title=Salford Red Devils' 2023 season in review|website=www.salfordnow.co.uk|date=2 October 2023 }}</ref>

On 7 August 2025 it was reported that he had left Salford.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|author=Callum Walker|date=7 Aug 2025|title=Ryan Brierley breaks silence following Salford Red Devils exit|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ryan-brierley-breaks-silence-following-salford-red-devils-exit/}}</ref>

===Oldham RLFC===
On 7 August 2025, Brierley signed a contract to join RFL Championship side Oldham.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ryan-brierleys-new-club-confirmed-following-salford-red-devils-exit/|title=Oldham sign Ryan Brierley after ‘heartbreaking’ Salford Red Devils exit|website=www.totalrl.com}}</ref>

==International career==
Though born in England, Brierley's family is from [[Kilwinning]] in [[North Ayrshire]], and thus he qualifies to play for [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] at international level.<ref>[http://www.totalrl.com/brierley-admits-scotland-debut-highlight-career/ Brierley admits Scotland début is highlight of his career]</ref> He made his Scotland début in the [[2016 Four Nations]], scoring a try in a defeat by [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]].

==Honours==
*'''[[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]]''': [[2014 RFL Championship|2014]], [[2015 RFL Championship|2015]]
*'''[[RFL League 1|League 1]]''': [[2017 League 1|2017]]
